title: Trigger
description:
A Trigger is what initiates an Operation or a Process — from a user, an external system, or the passage of time. Triggers are the entry point to all behavior.

A Trigger targets exactly one of: an Operation (ad-hoc invocation of a single Target.Action) or a Process (kick off the SOP from its startStep). The two are orthogonal — the same first-step Operation may have an Operation-level Trigger (standalone invocation) AND its containing Process may have a Process-level Trigger (full SOP kickoff).

Trigger replaces the prior 'Cause' primitive. Renamed for clarity and industry alignment (GitHub Actions, EventBridge, Zapier, n8n all use 'trigger').

operation.description: The Operation this trigger initiates, expressed as Target.Action (Target is any noun primitive). Mutually exclusive with `process`.

process.description: The Process this trigger initiates, by name. Process kickoff begins at the Process's startStep. Mutually exclusive with `operation`.

description.description: A human-readable explanation of what initiates this trigger.

schedule.description: A cron expression defining when the trigger fires. Required when source is 'schedule'.

event.description: The webhook event name that fires this trigger. Required when source is 'webhook'.

after.description: The Operation whose completion fires this trigger. Required when source is 'operation'.

condition.description: An optional condition that must be true at trigger time for the target to be initiated.

examples.description: Borrower submits a loan application form.
Kick off the full origination SOP when CRM signals a qualified lead.
